english: ba (ba), drin yzó-bai sems (drin gzo ba'i sems) gratitude, drin mi yzó-ba (drin mi gzo ba) ingratitude; drin-yzo-ċan (drin gzo can) grateful.

1. now, this moment, (opp. to dá ći (da ci), before, a little time ago) Mil.; at least just now, Mil.; da-yzod (da gzod) (-ċig (-cig)), id.; da-yzód bu yín-par ća yod (da gzod bu yin par cha yod) now I know that it is my son; not until now, then for the first time (in narratives with preterite tenses) Pth.; then at length Pth. - yzód-tsor-ba (gzod tshor ba), tos-pa (thos pa), -rdog-pa (-rdog pa) Dzl. to hear, to receive information, to be informed, to be told, yśégs-pa (gshegs pa) that he was gone Dzl. - 3. yzód-ma (gzod ma) beginning, commencement v. gdód-ma (gdod ma).

with čud (chud), v. čud (chud) and yzán-pa (gzan pa) extr.; bsgó-ba rnar yzón-pa (bsgo ba rnar gzon pa) the precept was wasted in the ear, it entered at one ear and left at the other; one Lex. gives the explanation: bsláb-bya-la mi nyán-pai don (bslab bya la mi nyan pa'i don).
1. Sch. quick, sharp, clever; caution, circumspection. - 2. v. yzáb-pa (gzab pa).
1. n. of a medicinal plant in Tibet Wdn. - 2. whatever is good, v. bzáṅ-ba (bzang ba). - 3. agreement, treaty, v. sgrig-pa (sgrig pa).

adj. and sbst., bzáṅ-po (bzang po) adj 1. good, in every respect, answering its purpose, excellent, suited, morally good; bsam-pa bzaṅ-po (bsam pa bzang po) a good resolution Mil.; bdag bzaṅ-na (bdag bzang na) if I behave well, keep myself free from blame, Do. (cf. légs-pa (legs pa)). - 2. fair, beautiful, as to the body, frq.; nags-tsal bzáṅ-po (nags tshal bzang po) a beautiful wood Mil.; yzugs-bzáṅ (gzugs bzang) of a fine, tall stature. - sbst.: bzaṅ (bzang) the good, that which is good in the abstract; bzáṅ-nas byuṅ (bzang nas byung) 'it came from good' i.e. from a good heart; dei yzáṅ-lan-du (de'i gzang lan du) as an acknowledgment of his goodness Glr. - Comp. bzáṅ-kyi (bzang kyi) a species of large dogs Cs. - bzaṅ-sgrig (bzang sgrig) treaty of peace, *jhe'-pa* (*jhe' pa*) C., *có-ċe* (*co ce*) W. to make peace, to come to an agreement, to conclude a treaty, frq.; bzaṅ-sgrig-pa (bzang sgrig pa) id. - bzaṅ-ṅán (bzang ngan) good and bad, good and ill, bzaṅ-ṅan-°briṅ ysum (bzang ngan 'bring gsum) good, bad, and indifferent; bzaṅ-ṅán °byéd-pa (bzang ngan 'byed pa) to discern between good and evil, to choose one or the other Schr.; bzaṅ-ṅán rtógs-pai sems (bzang ngan rtogs pa'i sems) is an attempt to find an adequate expression for the word 'conscience' Chr. P. - bzaṅ-tál (bzang thal) a good exit out of the °kór-ba ('khor ba) (the cycle of transmigrations), a happy departure Thgr. - bzaṅ-drúg (bzang drug) 'the six good things' (nutmeg, cloves, saffron, cardamom, camphor, sandal-wood) C.; used by Mil. also in a fig. sense; in W. simply: cloves. - bzaṅ-°dód (bzang 'dod) self-complacency. - bzaṅ-spyód (bzang spyod) 1. Cs. good action. 2. n. of a prayer of particular efficacy Glr., also called smón-lam-gyi rgyál-po (smon lam gyi rgyal po). - bzaṅ-btsón (bzang btson) v. btson (btson). - *zaṅ-lúg* (*zang lug*) W. good behaviour, good treatment, *mi żig-nẹ tób-ċe* (*mi zhig ne thob ce*) to experience such from a person, *mí-la có-ċe* (*mi la co ce*) to show it to a person.

only in kàṅ-bzáṅs (khang bzangs), which Wdn. explains by káṅ-pa brtségs-pa (khang pa brtsegs pa) a large house of several stories, applied only to the abodes of gods; in W. also the cubical part of the Chodten is called so.
rarely for bzod-pa (bzod pa); mi-bzád-pa (mi bzad pa) 1. intolerable Dzl., Do. - 2. irresistible Do.

sometimes for zan (zan), esp. food of animals, bzan °tsól-ba (bzan 'tshol ba) to seek food Mil.; pasture, pasturage, bzán-la skyél-ba (bzan la skyel ba) to place in pasture, to let feed Glr.; bzán-pa (bzan pa) Ts. id.

I. vb., fut. of zá-ba (za ba), to eat, bza (bza') this is to be eaten, in dietetic prescriptions; v. also zá-ba (za ba). - II. sbst. 1. (rarely yza (gza')) seems to denote the members of a family, they being conceived as eaters or fellow-boarders; bzá-tso máṅ-poi p'a-má (bza' tsho mang po'i pha ma) parents that have a large family Mil.; bza maṅs náṅ-na (bza' mangs nang na) among a numerous household Mil.; bza-drúg (bza' drug) a family, a company at table, of six persons, in f. C.; in certain combinations: wife, spouse, rgyá-mo bza (rgya mo bza') the Chinese spouse, bál-mo bza (bal mo bza') the Nepalese spouse (of the king), Glr. frq. - 2. meat, food, bzá-ba daṅ btúṅ-ba (bza' ba dang btung ba) meat and drink, specially the quality and quantity of food, zá- (za-)
